_meta.source: Joseph Myers, "2-uniform tilings with regular polygons and regular star polygons", August 2009
_meta.caution: p.1 verbatim: "Although the hand enumeration resulting in this list of 38 individual tilings and 5 infinite families (each determined by one variable angle) is intended to be complete, it has not been double-checked and should be treated with caution." — this oracle is itself an UNCHECKED hand enumeration; recovering it is 'first machine verification of an unchecked hand enumeration' (ST-3), not validation against proven ground truth.
_meta.transcribedBy: CC, 2026-06-10, from the PDF figure captions (Figs 1-43), per work order docs/review-2026-06-09/05-star-and-new-directions.md ST-3 step 1. Every orbit's corner angles were checked to sum to 2pi during transcription (point = alpha; dent interior = 2pi - 2pi/n - alpha). TA spot-check against the PDF captions PENDING (the work order requires it: a transcription error here becomes a false hard-fail or a silent pass).
_meta.tokenSyntax: StarVC token syntax (lib/classes/algorithm/StarVC.ts tokenStr / Polygon.cornerToken): bare `n` = regular n-gon corner; `n*p@u` = star POINT corner, interior angle u; `n*d@u` = star DENT corner, interior (reflex) angle u. CAUTION: the dent token's u is the dent's INTERIOR angle, NOT the star's point parameter — the star's point angle alpha = 24 - 24/n - u. All angles in pi/12 units (24 = full turn). Myers caption `n*_x` = point of star n with alpha = x; `n**_x` = dent of star n with alpha = x. Out-of-ring rational angles are exact fraction strings 'p/q'; family parameters are symbolic linear expressions in 'a' (= alpha in pi/12 units): 'a', 'a+2', '16-a'. Orbits are dot-joined token sequences in the caption's cyclic order.
_meta.alphaU: per-record map: star species (keyed by its Myers caption label) -> point angle alpha in pi/12 units. Integer for in-ring; exact fraction string for out-of-ring; symbolic string for families.
_meta.inRingRule: true iff every tile lives in the N=24 ring: regular n-gon with interior angle a multiple of pi/12 (n in {3,4,6,8,12,24}) and stars n*_alpha with n | 24 and alpha a positive integer multiple of pi/12 (then the dent angle 24 - 24/n - alpha is integral too). Families have free alpha and are inRing: false as catalogue objects; they enter a run only as pinned in-ring representatives (ST-4 / TH-8 semantics).
_meta.pinsRationale: ST-3 step 3 regression pins: in-ring k=2 tilings with one purely-regular orbit. They are the falsifiers for the unscoped Myers prune (iii) ('every star-tiling vertex carries >= 1 star point'), which is sound at k=1 only — at k=2 a whole vertex ORBIT can be star-free. Any future k>=2 star VC/seed layer must be able to represent these records; the prune rescope itself is TH-5.
observations: Tilings with at least one purely-regular orbit: Figs 36, 38, 39, 40, 41, 42, 43 + family Fig 25 (second orbit 3.3.3.3.3.3). NOTE: the ST-3 work-order evidence lists 'Figs 36, 38-42 + family Fig 25' — Fig 43 (4.4.8*_pi/4.4.8*_pi/4; 4.4.4.4) ALSO has a purely-regular orbit and appears to have been omitted there; the pin set 36/40/42 is unaffected.

FIVE one-parameter families exist at k=2 (Figs 25-28, 32) — this falsifies the as-stated conjecture 'parametric families exist only at k=1' (star-parametric-roadmap-2026-06-05.md §4), modulo Myers's own caution. The reframed open question (ST-3 step 4, AL decision): do PRIMITIVE families exist at k>=2, i.e. not derived from a k=1 family by orbit refinement? First test: is Fig 25 (3.3.3*_a.3**_a; 3^6) derived from the k=1 family 3.3*_a.3.3**_a?
All four out-of-ring tilings (Figs 18, 19, 22, 23) involve 9-, 18-fold stars or alpha in pi/9-multiples — out of reach of the N=24 backend by the ring, not by the method (ST-5 scope ruling).
